atlas_title: A prenatal skin atlas reveals immune regulation of human skin morphogenesis
description: One of four macrophage subsets identified in prenatal human skin (alongside LYVE1+, TML, and iron-recycling macrophages). Annotated using marker genes from Suo et al. (2022). Characterized by high expression of MHC class II genes (HLA-DPA1, HLA-DRA) and CLEC7A, as well as typical macrophage markers F13A1, CD163, and SPP1. Lacks Langerhans cell markers CD207 and CD1A. Also referred to as MHCIIhi in Suo et al. (2022).
notes: The MHCII+ macrophage label in the prenatal skin atlas directly corresponds to the MHC class II hi macrophage subset first defined in the cross-organ developmental immune atlas by Suo et al. (2022). The Gopee et al. (2024) atlas adopted the same macrophage subset annotation scheme.
